vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to engine heat.
risk.mit: Implement active cooling systems in avionics bay.

risk.desc: Structural integrity concerns under extreme loading conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough FEA analysis and reinforce critical areas.

risk.desc: Limited accessibility for maintenance purposes.
risk.mit: Ensure sufficient access doors and clear pathways.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.path: Wing spar loads -> Fuselage spine -> Landing gear mounts
Engine heat -> Avionics bay cooling fins -> Ambient air
Nacelle tilt mechanism -> Bearing seats -> Fuselage spine
Avionics bay power -> Harness passthrough -> Wing spar socket
Human access -> Avionics bay maintenance door -> Avionics bay

integration_opportunities.details: Add heat sink fins and airflow ducts
Evaluate material thickness and reinforcement patterns

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: ±0.5mm for critical interfaces, ±1mm for non-critical features
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for overhangs during FDM, toolpaths for CNC machining
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment, dimensional checks using CMM

verification_focus.check: Finite element analysis (FEA) on wing spar loads and landing gear forces
Thermal imaging of avionics bay under simulated engine operation
Verify human access to avionics bay and camera window

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on wing spar due to heat dissipation from fuselage spine.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management solutions such as heat sinks or cooling channels.

risk.desc: Risk of improper alignment during assembly due to complex interface requirements.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and conduct pre-assembly checks.

risk.desc: Service access may be limited in tight spaces, hindering maintenance.
risk.mit: Optimize service access design to ensure easy access for maintenance tasks.

risk.desc: Structural integrity may be compromised if wing spar loads are not properly managed.
risk.mit: Conduct rigorous structural testing and validate load paths.

risk.desc: Fastener locking and preload may not be sufficient under dynamic conditions.
risk.mit: Use high-strength fasteners and implement additional locking mechanisms.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment due to tolerance stack-up in the tilt axis.
risk.mit: Implement a tolerance analysis and adjust manufacturing tolerances accordingly.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling efficiency if heat sink fins are obstructed.
risk.mit: Ensure clearances around the cooling fins and conduct thermal testing.

risk.desc: Risk of mechanical failure if the servo linkage is not properly secured.
risk.mit: Include locking washers and specify torque values for all fasteners.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference if wiring channels are not properly routed.
risk.mit: Conduct electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) testing and optimize wiring layout.

risk.desc: Risk of structural integrity issues if the bearing seats are not adequately reinforced.
risk.mit: Review stress analysis results and reinforce bearing seat areas as needed.
